note: Per-question fact rubric for the reference-catalog eval. FAIRNESS RULE: every independent:true question grades ONLY a fact whose ground truth exists in the real Fowler/Kerievsky books (pattern MEMBERSHIP in a refactoring's row, a real 1st->2nd-ed rename/merge, a real smell provenance, a real print-vs-web provenance). NO direction word (away/toward/towards/de-pattern/dismantle/retreat) is ever a required or forbidden grade token. mustName.all = AND list (each must appear affirmed); mustName.anyOf = array of OR-groups (each group needs >=1 affirmed hit); mustNotClaim = forbidden claims (fail if any appears un-negated). Matching is word-bounded + whitespace-tolerant + clause-scoped BIDIRECTIONAL negation-aware, with hedged-contrastive retraction (see grade-reference.mjs). A target with an 'excluded_from_clean_core' field is SKILL.md-echo-tainted: it is kept for grader coverage but is NOT part of the clean-core run (q3, q5). q2 and q4 were dropped from the suite entirely (saturated / SKILL.md-echo).

targets.fact: Move Accumulation to Visitor sits in two pattern rows: it heads toward Visitor and steps back from Iterator. Iterator is the discriminator (base may name only the pattern in the title).

targets.excluded_from_clean_core: SKILL.md-echo: the discriminator 'Inline Singleton' appears verbatim in the SKILL.md body (step 4 Away-refactoring list), so invoke_skill answers from the injected preamble, not the catalog. Retained for grader coverage; NOT in the clean-core run.
targets.fact: The named Kerievsky move for an unwarranted Singleton is Inline Singleton; a plain functional alternative is a module namespace / module of functions.

targets.excluded_from_clean_core: SKILL.md-echo: 'Combinatorial Explosion' appears in the SKILL.md body routed to a Kerievsky pattern-directed refactoring, so invoke_skill answers 'Kerievsky' from the injected preamble, not the catalog. Retained for grader-fix coverage; NOT in the clean-core run.
targets.fact: Combinatorial Explosion is a Kerievsky-unique smell (Refactoring to Patterns Ch.4), not a Fowler smell.
Primitive Obsession is catalogued by both Fowler and Kerievsky.
Fowler generalized 1st-ed Introduce Null Object into 2nd-ed Introduce Special Case.
One 2nd-ed refactoring, Change Function Declaration, absorbed Add Parameter + Remove Parameter + Rename Method.
Return Modified Value is in Fowler's online catalog only, not in the printed 2nd edition.

anyOf: not in the second edition print

targets.fact: Fowler's 2nd ed has NO Extract Interface refactoring; the case maps to Change Function Declaration / Extract Superclass.

anyOf: does not include an Extract Interface
does not have an Extract Interface
does not contain an Extract Interface

targets.fact: The 1st-ed Consolidate Duplicate Conditional Fragments case is covered by 2nd-ed Slide Statements (a real 2nd-ed rename/merge).
Indecent Exposure is a Kerievsky-unique smell, not a Fowler smell and not absent from both.
